index.cipher_description: Test your browser's data isolation layer below. Type any sensitive payload string. The engine will instantly scramble the input locally inside your client environment, demonstrating how data arrays are structurally blinded before hitting our distributed database shards.
index.anonymity_description: Anonymity is designed directly into the core of the Citadel Network. By allowing you to post only your desired information, utilizing alias names, and handling encrypted saving operations, your data is yours. We designed this for the people, by the people.
index.nzk_description: Our models are designed and coded directly on a Near Zero-Knowledge (N-NZK) design. In another words, we utilize a lot of mathematical operations for encryption and decryption and have your browser handle the encryption before it is sent out over the network.
index.mesh_description: Anonymized telemetry tracks local interactions, masks sessions and IP blocks, then safely sends batched events via beacons to locked, isolated backend logs. This allows for the team to investigate unusual activities, bug bounty programs, and auditing the site.
index.multi_description: Multi-lingual interface core providing native localization across primary languages via an isolated translation matrix. Language vector mutations execute seamlessly using sanitized session state switching and zero-telemetry, fail-safe client redirection.
index.usr_description: Inside the Citadel, your digital footprint is invisible by default. Other users cannot inspect your profile, track your posts, or intercept your inbox without an established handshake (Friends). Absolute privacy, enforced by design.
index.data_description: Here at MyCitadel, we will never show you ads. Therefor, there is zero requirements or needs for tracking your voice modules, geo-graphic location, harvesting cookies, or any other means of tracking your data on other websites like our competitors.

about.history_description_p1: MyCitadel was engineered not as a commercial product, but as a mandatory digital sanctuary. The modern social web has devolved into an active surveillance dragnet where user location vectors are tracked continuously, ambient voice data is harvested to trigger target consumer feeds, and algorithms explicitly exploit human vulnerability for profit. For families seeking shelter from digital exposure, the mainstream infrastructure is fundamentally broken.
about.history_description_p2: Faced with the urgent necessity of providing an uncompromised, default-deny communication perimeter where children can interact safely without exposure to automated spam networks, malicious actors, or persistent digital tracking, the architecture of the Citadel was drafted. It bypasses corporate cloud choke points completely, implementing client-side mathematical sovereignty to protect the human elements that matter most.
about.architect_meta_p1: The primary cryptographic engineer behind MyCitadel is Brogan Cole Gallagher. Holding a Ph.D. in Computer Science, alongside elite defensive credentials including the CEH (EC-Council) and OSCP (OffSec), Brogan's technical mastery was forged through a horrific, punishing upbringing. Surviving a childhood weaponized by severe physical abuse, systematic neglect, and catastrophic identity theft—where his own parents stole his information to open lines of credit while he was still a minor—he learned early that survival required absolute self-reliance. When family members stole funds from one child and pinned the blame on Brogan to incite bitter, lifelong sibling rages, and his own father explicitly told him he should have been buried in the ground instead of his grandfather, Brogan realized his biological bloodline was an active threat vector. He achieved academic and professional excellence entirely on his own terms, despite a family that demanded he constantly grind his way out of poverty while funding their demands and watching them shower toxic favoritism on others.
about.architect_meta_p2: The breaking point arrived when Brogan's foundational publications, scientific research papers, and early corporate ventures under his birth name (Aubrey W. Love II) completely dominated global search metrics. His father, fueled by professional jealousy because his own dream of retiring to write books was buried at the bottom of the Google search index, angrily demanded Brogan cease operations because he couldn't escape his son's spotlight. Compounding this systemic betrayal, a 16-year marriage collapsed into further exploitation; despite providing private financial support and daily coordination for his four amazing children, his ex-wife repeatedly violated their divorce decree, retreating into a rent-free safety net with her family while leaving Brogan to single-handedly struggle to make ends meet. Realizing he was entirely alone against a world of parasitic exploitation, Brogan executed a permanent identity transition. Legally changing his name and relocating to the rugged independence of Wyoming, he cut the dead weight of a selfish family to forge an un-compromised future.
about.architect_meta_p3: Out of the ash of absolute isolation, a profound turning point emerged. What began as a casual TikTok joke materialized into the baseline of Brogan's survival: Rebecca Files. Making the fierce commitment to relocate from Kansas to Texas to stand by his side, Rebecca stepped directly into the crossfire of his past. Though the fallout was brutal and the psychological weight heavy, she fought through every storm with unyielding loyalty. Where an entire lifetime of family and relationships sought to destroy and exploit him, Rebecca brought a defiant light, absolute peace, comfort, and undeniable joy. She earned her place in the absolute inner circle of his life—proving that even in a world of universal deceit, a fiercely protected sanctuary is still possible.
about.anti_surveillance_p1: The structural blueprint of MyCitadel is deeply personal, designed by a father who had to learn the hard way that no one else was coming to protect his inner circle. This platform was custom-built as an offensive counter-measure against scammers, stalkers, and predators, completely stripping away their ability to target his daughters. By implementing a strict, default-deny architecture, no connection can ever be initiated or forced upon his children unless the endpoint user explicitly authorizes the link.
about.anti_surveillance_p2: By utilizing localized, client-side cryptographic operations, MyCitadel establishes an unbreakable, un-interceptable communication bridge. It guarantees his daughters a private, highly secure gateway to message their father directly from their mother's house, completely insulated from corporate data harvesting, parental domestic interference, or outside noise. This is defensive engineering born from real-world survival—built by a father, secured by a battle-tested specialist, and hardened to protect the people who matter most.
about.pipeline_description: The system executes on a hardened Near Zero-Knowledge (N-NZK) framework. Data processing runs local client-side mathematical operations directly inside your browser container before network transit occurs.

terms.header_desc: This document defines your sovereign rights as a Citadel Citizen, the conduct code governing this network, how to protect your anonymity, and the authorized parameters for security researchers and penetration testers.
terms.header_strong: Read it. Understand it. Own it.
terms.art1_short_desc: MyCitadel was built on a single, uncompromising belief:
terms.slogan1: If you do not pay for a product,
terms.art1_desc1: Every major social network currently operating generates its revenue by harvesting your behavioral data, building psychographic profiles, and selling access to your attention to the highest bidder. Your photos, your political opinions, your private messages, your relationships, your location history — all of it is aggregated, packaged, and sold. You are not a user. You are inventory.
terms.art1_desc2: MyCitadel refuses this model entirely. You pay a modest subscription — or use a free tier with meaningful functionality — and in exchange, we owe you complete transparency and architectural accountability. We do not run ads. We do not sell data. We do not partner with analytics brokers. We do not cooperate with surveillance programs. We build tools that make it cryptographically impossible for us to betray you, because the architecture itself prevents it.
terms.guarantee_desc: Our Near-Zero-Knowledge (N-NZK) model means that your posts, messages, media, and personal content are encrypted inside your browser before they ever leave your device. By the time data reaches our servers, it is already an opaque ciphertext block. We store it. We cannot read it.
terms.art2_desc: When you register on MyCitadel, you do not become a user account in a corporate database. You become a Citizen — a sovereign node within the Citadel network. These rights are guaranteed to you unconditionally and cannot be waived by us or revoked without just cause under our Conduct Code.
terms.anon_desc: You may register using any alias you choose. We do not require your legal name, government ID, phone number, or any personally identifying information. Your Alias is your Citadel identity.
terms.sovereign_desc: Your encryption keys are derived from your passphrase on your device. We do not hold, store, or have access to your master key. You own your data layer.
terms.portability_desc: We can not just hand over any data of any specific user. We can manage to search by alias name, however, without their unique identification phrase, decrypting the information is impossible to crack.
terms.delete_desc: Deleting your account triggers a full cascade wipe: profile, posts, comments, connections, messages, and media are all permanently destroyed. When a connection is severed between two Citizens, all shared communications are mutually destroyed. No backups are retained after 72 hours.
terms.rtk: Right to Know What We Know
terms.rtk_desc: At any time, you may request a transparency report detailing exactly what information MyCitadel holds about you in plaintext form. Spoiler: it is minimal by design.
terms.appeal_desc: If your account is actioned by our moderation system, you have the right to appeal the decision via appeals@mycitadel.lol. Appeals are reviewed by a human administrator within 72 hours. Please ensure you do so PRIOR to account deletion or other abrasive actions.
terms.art3_desc: MyCitadel is a sovereign network, not a free-for-all. The Citadel's strength is its community. The following code governs all Citizen activity across all surfaces — posts, comments, messages, profile content, group activity, and any other interaction vector.
terms.express_desc: Share opinions, ideas, creative works, commentary, news, art, and discourse on any topic that does not violate the prohibited vectors below. MyCitadel does not censor political viewpoints, ideological perspectives, or unpopular opinions.
terms.connections_desc: Send connection requests to any Citizen. Accept or decline freely. Sever connections at any time with full mutual data destruction. Block any Citizen without explanation required.
terms.comms_desc: All direct messages between connected Citizens are end-to-end encrypted using a unique connection key pair. Use this channel freely and privately for personal communications.
terms.reporting_desc: If you encounter content that violates this Conduct Code, use the in-platform report function. Reports are reviewed by our moderation system and human administrators.
terms.multiaccount_desc: Citizens may maintain separate alias identities for different contexts (personal, professional, creative). Each account must independently comply with this Conduct Code.
terms.speech_desc: Content that incites violence, dehumanizes individuals or groups based on protected characteristics (race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, national origin), or constitutes targeted harassment, doxxing, or coordinated abuse campaigns.
terms.child: Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM) & Child Exploitation
terms.child_desc: Any content that sexualizes minors, facilitates grooming, or endangers children in any capacity. This is an absolute zero-tolerance prohibition. All confirmed instances are reported to NCMEC and relevant law enforcement immediately — this is the one area where our N-NZK model yields to legal obligation without exception.
terms.terror_desc: Content that plans, promotes, glorifies, recruits for, or provides material support to acts of terrorism, mass violence, or organized criminal enterprises targeting individuals or groups.
terms.fraud_desc: Impersonating another Citizen or real-world person for deceptive purposes, operating financial scams, phishing campaigns, or cryptocurrency fraud schemes targeting other Citizens.
terms.spam_desc: Operating bots, running automated posting scripts, mass-messaging Citizens without consent, or artificially inflating engagement metrics using non-human methods.
terms.attacks_desc: Attempting to compromise the Citadel server, exploit vulnerabilities for unauthorized access (rather than responsible disclosure), launch DDoS attacks, or tamper with other Citizens' encrypted data streams. See Article IX for the authorized research path.
terms.sharing_desc: Sharing, distributing, or threatening to distribute intimate imagery of any person without their explicit, documented, ongoing consent.
terms.art4_desc: MyCitadel provides the infrastructure for anonymity — but anonymity is a practice, not just a feature. The following guidance will help you maximize your operational security as a Citizen.
terms.art4_imp_desc: MyCitadel encrypts your data at rest and in transit. But we cannot protect you from yourself. If you post your real name, address, workplace, or face, that information is now public — and encrypted at the database level does not undo voluntary disclosure. However, NO ONE is capable of seeing your data WITHOUT having an ESTABLISHED connection with you first.
terms.pwd_desc: Your Citadel passphrase is used to derive your encryption keys locally. This means it never leaves your device — but it also means we cannot recover it. Use a passphrase you will remember. A minimum of 20 characters combining random words, numbers, and symbols is strongly recommended. Consider a password manager. If you lose your passphrase, your encrypted data is unrecoverable.
terms.tfa_desc: MyCitadel supports TOTP-based 2FA via any authenticator app (Aegis, Bitwarden Authenticator, etc.). Enabling 2FA earns you a Reputation badge and significantly hardens your account against credential attacks.
terms.email_desc: Do not register with your primary personal email address. Consider a privacy-focused email provider (ProtonMail, Tutanota, SimpleLogin) or a purpose-created alias for your Citadel registration.
terms.anonymity_desc: MyCitadel does not log IP addresses beyond rate-limiting requirements. However, for maximum network anonymity, consider accessing the Citadel via a trusted VPN or Tor Browser. Be aware that Tor may impact performance on media-heavy pages.
terms.uploading_desc: Photos taken on modern smartphones embed EXIF metadata — including GPS coordinates, device model, and timestamp. Strip EXIF data before uploading sensitive imagery. Tools like ExifTool (free, open-source) or your phone's built-in privacy settings can do this automatically.
terms.fingerprinting: Be Mindful of Writing Style Fingerprinting
terms.fingerprinting_desc: Sophisticated adversaries can de-anonymize writers based on unique linguistic patterns, vocabulary choices, and sentence structure. If your threat model requires high-assurance anonymity, be conscious of how you write.
terms.devices_desc: MyCitadel sessions are cryptographically secured, but session tokens stored in a browser on a shared or compromised device remain a physical access risk. Always log out fully when using non-personal devices.
terms.art5_desc: Transparency is foundational. Here is a precise accounting of what MyCitadel stores, why, and how it is protected.
terms.alias_desc: Stored in plaintext to enable Citizens page search, public profiles, and connection requests. Choose your alias carefully — it is the only truly public piece of your identity.
terms.profile_desc: Non-sensitive categorical fields (relationship status, etc.) that you opt in to displaying on your public profile card. These are voluntary and can be left blank.
terms.date_desc: Stored for account health and moderation purposes. Last Active is granular to the day, not the hour.
terms.email_adr_desc: Your registration email is stored as a bcrypt hash — we can verify it for password reset flows, but we cannot read it, export it, or use it for marketing purposes.
terms.posts: All Posts & Timeline Content Encrypted client-side before submission. Stored as ciphertext. We hold the blob; you hold the key.
terms.messages: All Direct Messages End-to-end encrypted per connection key pair. Neither MyCitadel nor any third party can intercept message content.
terms.media: All Media Uploads Photos, videos, and file attachments are encrypted before upload. Stored in an out-of-web-root directory as encrypted blobs.
terms.profile_bio: Profile Bio & Extended Personal Details All freeform text fields in your profile are encrypted. Only connected Citizens with the appropriate connection key can read them.
terms.comments: Comments & Reactions Encrypted at submission alongside the post they reference.
terms.subpoena_desc: If we receive a legally valid subpoena for user data, our response will be a database dump of the above. Alias names, a hashed email, timestamps, and encrypted ciphertext blobs with no keys attached. Decrypting that payload without the citizen's passphrase-derived key is computationally infeasible. We will notify affected Citizens of any subpoena we receive, to the full extent permitted by applicable law.
terms.art6_desc: Content posted to MyCitadel remains yours. By posting, you grant MyCitadel a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license solely to store and transmit that content to your authorized connections. We claim no ownership over your content and will never use it for advertising, training AI models, or any commercial purpose.
terms.mod_desc: Because most content is encrypted, our AI moderation layer operates at submission time only — scanning the cleartext payload for policy violations before your browser encrypts and transmits it. We do not store the cleartext after this check. This is a deliberate and disclosed architectural decision.
terms.ai_mod_desc: MyCitadel uses an AI moderation layer that scans content at submission time — before encryption — to detect policy violations in real time. When a violation is detected, the following process occurs:
terms.first_desc: The submission is blocked. A detailed warning is displayed explaining which policy was triggered and why. The Citizen receives 1 Fraction on their record. The content is not published. No permanent action is taken.
terms.second_desc: The submission is blocked again. A final warning is issued notifying the Citizen that one more Fraction will result in permanent account termination. Total Fractions: 2 of 3.
terms.third_desc: The account is permanently destroyed. Profile, posts, connections, messages, and media are purged in a full cascade wipe. Active Premium subscriptions are immediately cancelled with no refund. There is no reinstatement after 3 Fractions.
terms.fraction: Fractions are not issued for borderline content — only clear, confirmed policy violations. False positive determinations can be appealed via appeals@mycitadel.lol within 24 hours of issuance.
terms.public: Public profile & Citadel Citizens page access
terms.pcr: Post, comment, react to public content
terms.analytics: Advanced analytics on your own content
terms.stripe_desc: Payments are processed via Stripe using industry-standard TLS encryption. MyCitadel does not store your payment card details — Stripe handles all card data under their PCI-DSS compliance program. You may cancel your Premium subscription at any time from your account settings. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing cycle. No refunds are issued for partial billing periods, except where required by applicable consumer protection law in your jurisdiction.
terms.art9_desc: MyCitadel was built by a security professional and we deeply respect the security research community. We believe the best way to build a genuinely secure system is to empower skilled researchers to attack it — within defined boundaries. This is our authorized research charter.
terms.safe_harbor_desc: Security research conducted in good faith, within the authorized scope defined below, is protected under this Safe Harbor. We will not pursue civil or criminal action against researchers who comply with this charter. We will respond to all valid reports within 72 hours.
terms.audit_desc: Test the correctness and security of our Libsodium XChaCha20-Poly1305 implementation. Verify key derivation procedures, nonce handling, and ciphertext integrity. Demonstrate any weaknesses in our browser-side encryption layer.
terms.auth_desc: Attempt to bypass login controls, session fixation, session hijacking, token replay attacks, TOTP bypass, or broken authentication flows using your own registered test accounts.
terms.injection_desc: SQL injection, XSS (reflected, stored, DOM-based), command injection, SSTI, and SSRF against all input surfaces on the platform. Verify our DOMPurify sanitization layer holds.
terms.access_desc: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object References), privilege escalation, broken access controls allowing one Citizen to access another's encrypted data, or admin panel exposure.
terms.web_root_desc: Verify that encrypted media files are stored outside the web root and are inaccessible via direct URL traversal, path traversal, or LFI exploits.
terms.ai_bypass_desc: Attempt to bypass the client-side AI moderation layer to submit policy-violating content without triggering a Fraction. Report successful bypass vectors — do not exploit them.
terms.tls_desc: Evaluate CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, CORS policy, TLS cipher suite strength, and certificate configuration. Use tools like SSL Labs, securityheaders.com, and Observatory by Mozilla.
terms.rate_desc: Test whether authentication endpoints, API calls, and registration flows are properly rate-limited against brute force, credential stuffing, and enumeration attacks.
terms.real_accounts_desc: You may only test against accounts you personally own and created for research purposes. Accessing, modifying, or exfiltrating any real Citizen's data — even to demonstrate a vulnerability — is strictly prohibited.
terms.ddos_desc: No DDoS, load testing, resource exhaustion, database corruption, or any action designed to degrade service availability for other Citizens. Demonstrate vulnerabilities — do not weaponize them.
terms.sec_desc: Phishing, vishing, or any social engineering targeting MyCitadel staff, administrators, or other Citizens. Physical premises intrusion.
terms.third_party_desc: Attacking hosting providers, DNS providers (Namecheap), payment processors (Stripe), or any third-party service we depend on. Their security programs govern testing on their infrastructure.
terms.automated_scanners_desc: Aggressive automated scanners (Nikto, Nessus, Burp active scan at high intensity) run without prior notification can impact service availability. Contact us before running automated tooling. Light reconnaissance scanning is acceptable.
terms.public_publishing_desc: Do not publish, tweet, post, or otherwise disclose vulnerability details before we have had the opportunity to patch and notify affected Citizens. We ask for a 90-day coordinated disclosure window from initial report. We will acknowledge your report publicly when you wish.
terms.submit_report_desc: All vulnerability reports must include: a clear description of the vulnerability class, steps to reproduce (ideally with a PoC), the potential impact, affected endpoints or components, and your suggested remediation if you have one. Screenshots and video walkthroughs are welcome.
terms.rewards_desc: MyCitadel is a bootstrapped startup. We offer Citadel Reputation Points in lieu of monetary rewards during our early-access phase, alongside public acknowledgement in our Hall of Defenders. As the platform grows, cash rewards for Critical and High severity findings will be introduced.
terms.crit_desc: Full auth bypass, RCE, mass data exposure of citizen ciphertext + keys
terms.high_desc: IDOR exposing citizen data, Stored XSS, SQLi, Privilege escalation.
terms.med_desc: Reflected XSS, CSRF, Rate limit bypass, Sensitive info disclosure.
terms.low_desc: Missing security headers, TLS config issues, Minor info leaks.
terms.art10_desc: In the event of a confirmed security breach, MyCitadel will execute the following response protocol:
terms.citizen_alert_desc: All affected Citizens will be notified via their registered (hashed) email and an in-platform banner. The notification will detail: what was exposed, when it occurred, what we have done, and what Citizens should do (e.g., rotate passphrase).
terms.reauth_desc: All active sessions will be invalidated immediately upon breach confirmation. Citizens will be required to re-authenticate and are strongly advised to rotate their passphrases.
terms.post_incident_desc: A full post-incident analysis will be published on our status page within 14 days detailing the root cause, impact scope, and remediation steps taken. We believe in radical transparency in failure.
terms.unauth_access_desc: Unauthorized access to another Citizen's account, unauthorized extraction of encrypted data stores, or deliberate attempts to enumerate Citizen alias names in bulk are treated as criminal offenses under applicable computer fraud statutes (CFAA, Computer Misuse Act, etc.) and will be reported to relevant cyber-crime authorities. Our server logs — while minimal — are preserved for forensic purposes.
terms.art11_desc1: MyCitadel is operated as an independent platform. These terms are governed by applicable law in the jurisdiction of operation. By registering, you agree that disputes will be resolved via good-faith negotiation first, followed by binding arbitration if necessary.
terms.art11_desc2: We will comply with all legally valid subpoenas, court orders, and law enforcement requests. As detailed in Article V, what we can produce in response to such orders is structurally limited by our architecture: alias names, hashed emails, timestamps, and encrypted ciphertext blobs for which we do not hold decryption keys. We will challenge overly broad or legally deficient requests. We are not in the business of building surveillance infrastructure.
terms.art12_desc: When this document is materially revised, all Citizens will receive an in-platform notification at least 14 days before the new version takes effect. The document integrity hash displayed at the top of this page changes with every revision, providing a verifiable audit trail. Continued use of MyCitadel after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the revised terms. If you disagree with a material change, you may delete your account before the effective date and receive a prorated refund of any unused Premium subscription time.
terms.claim_desc: By creating a Citadel Node, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to operate within this Protocol. Your anonymity is yours to protect. We give you the architecture. You provide the discipline.
